# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1070
- **Lines of code**: 346,504
- **Languages**: TypeScript (902), CSS (44), JavaScript (42), Markdown (32), JSON (25)
- **Zones**: 32 (32 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-09T00:45:04.419Z

## Architecture Zones

### Architecture Docs (`architecture-docs`)

Project documentation covering architecture decisions, analysis reports, and operational guides — entirely static Markdown with no import graph edges.

- Files: 19
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/architecture-docs/context.md`

### CLI End-to-End Tests (`cli-e2e-tests`)

End-to-end test suite that exercises the n-dx orchestration CLI commands, delegation contracts, architecture policies, and error paths against real spawned processes.

- Files: 22
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-e2e-tests/context.md`

### Cross-Package Integration Tests (`cross-package-integration-tests`)

Integration test suite that verifies cross-package contracts and scheduler startup behavior at the monorepo boundary.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cross-package-integration-tests/context.md`

### Hench Agent (`hench-agent`)

The autonomous agent package responsible for task selection, brief generation, LLM tool-use loops, run recording, and all supporting store and analysis infrastructure.

- Files: 157
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-agent/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

**/api/** (handleIntegrationRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-integrations.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/integrations/:id/config
GET     /api/integrations
GET     /api/integrations/:id/config
GET     /api/integrations/:id/schema
PUT     /api/integrations/:id/config
```

**/api/notion/** (handleNotionRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-notion.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/notion/config
GET     /api/notion/config
POST    /api/notion/schema
POST    /api/notion/schema/fix
POST    /api/notion/test
PUT     /api/notion/config
```

**/api/project** (handleProjectRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-project.ts`

```
GET     /api/project
```

**/api/search** (handleSearchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-search.ts`

```
GET     /api/search?q=<query>&limit=N
```

**/api/sv/** (handleSourcevisionRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-sourcevision.ts`

```
GET     /api/sv/components
GET     /api/sv/context
GET     /api/sv/imports
GET     /api/sv/inventory
GET     /api/sv/manifest
GET     /api/sv/pr-markdown
GET     /api/sv/summary
GET     /api/sv/zones
```

**/api/status** (handleStatusRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-status.ts`

```
GET     /api/status
```

**/api/token/** (handleTokenUsageRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-token-usage.ts`

```
GET     /api/token/budget
GET     /api/token/by-command
GET     /api/token/by-period
GET     /api/token/events
GET     /api/token/summary
```

**/api/rex/validate** (handleValidationRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-validation.ts`

```
GET     /api/rex/dependency-graph
GET     /api/rex/validate
```

**/api/hench/workflow/** (handleWorkflowRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-workflow.ts`

```
GET     /api/hench/workflow/analysis
GET     /api/hench/workflow/history
POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- The architecture-policy test enforces gateway and tier-boundary rules via static source analysis but has no runtime assertion that gateway files contain only re-exports and no logic; a gateway that wraps a function with added behavior (e.g. logging, error transformation) passes all static checks while silently violating the re-export-only contract. [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- The concurrency contract table in CLAUDE.md documents 9 unsafe command pairs, but scheduler-startup.test.js only validates server startup sequencing — no test exercises concurrent writes to .rex/prd.json or .sourcevision/ to verify data-corruption prevention; the contract is documentation-only with zero automated regression. [warning] (cross-package-integration-tests)
- The rex-gateway.ts re-exports 8 functions but no automated check verifies that all 8 are actually consumed by hench. Stale re-exports silently widen the declared dependency surface. domain-isolation.test.js enforces 're-export only' but not 'export only what is used', leaving gateway minimality unenforced. [info] (hench-agent)
- Two distinct Louvain communities are both assigned the zone ID 'hench-agent': the 155-file main zone (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) and the 14-file cross-package residual (cohesion 0.33, coupling 0.67). Duplicate zone IDs make health metrics ambiguous — tooling that aggregates or thresholds by ID silently blends metrics from two structurally incompatible groups. The residual zone requires a distinct ID before its metrics can be acted upon. [warning] (hench-agent)
- The config.js spawn-exempt rule (permitted to read JSON config files directly, forbidden from importing TypeScript package modules) has no automated enforcement. The architecture-policy e2e test verifies the four-tier import hierarchy generally, but config.js's specific exception boundary is untested — a package module import added to config.js would pass CI undetected. [warning] (monorepo-root)
- prd.json is written by hench-agent (task status updates), monorepo-root (plan operations), and web MCP handlers without any file-level write coordinator. CLAUDE.md documents 'data corruption risk' for concurrent plan+work writes but provides no code-level mitigation — an advisory lock file or write-queue module is entirely absent. This is a missing abstraction layer for a shared mutable resource with multiple concurrent writers. [critical] (rex-runtime-data)
- No automated assertion verifies that all 21 outgoing cross-zone calls flow through the two gateway files and none bypass them via direct leaf-to-leaf imports; a new developer adding a cross-package import in a non-gateway file would pass all existing tests undetected until sourcevision re-analysis. [warning] (web-dashboard)
- The 'web' residual zone (cohesion 0.33, 14 files) has persisted across at least three analysis passes without resolution, indicating zone hints for viewer components and build scripts are not being applied or are being overridden by Louvain; unresolved residual zones silently invalidate health metrics for both web-dashboard and the residual zone indefinitely. [warning] (web-dashboard)
- God function: generateStructuralInsights in packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Documentation-to-code semantic dependency: architecture-docs governs gateway rules and tier boundaries that are enforced in hench-agent (rex-gateway.ts) and web-viewer (domain-gateway.ts), but this relationship is invisible to the import graph. Drift between docs and implementation can only be caught by the CI freshness check, not by zone health metrics. [info] (architecture-docs)
- The MCP HTTP transport path (ndx start → /mcp/rex, /mcp/sourcevision endpoints) has no e2e test coverage; the stdio transport path is validated indirectly through delegation tests, creating an asymmetric risk as HTTP becomes the recommended transport. [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- No integration test covers the hench→rex gateway contract; hench is the primary autonomous consumer of rex at runtime, yet rex-gateway.ts re-export stability is unvalidated in this zone — API drift between rex releases would go undetected until a hench runtime failure. [warning] (cross-package-integration-tests)
- The cross-package residual zone (cohesion 0.33, coupling 0.67) containing both packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts and packages/web/ files is a zone detection artifact with the highest coupling score in the batch. Coupling 0.67 does not represent real architectural coupling — it is an artifact of Louvain grouping unrelated files. Zone health metrics for this entry are unreliable until decomposition is applied. [info] (hench-agent)
- hench-agent → rex boundary follows the gateway pattern (rex-gateway.ts), creating a single auditable import edge between execution and domain tiers. This is the only directed production import crossing from execution tier downward in the entire zone graph. [info] (hench-agent)
- The 'web' residual zone (cohesion: 0.33, 14 files) co-existing with the 'web-dashboard' zone (cohesion: 0.98, 391 files) indicates incomplete zone membership for the web package — files stranded in the residual zone make health metrics for both zones unreliable. [warning] (web-dashboard)

### Relationships

- architecture-policy.test.js and the cross-package-integration-tests zone together form complementary enforcement layers (static import rules + runtime wiring), but the gap between them — runtime gateway shape correctness — is uncovered by either zone. [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- The two-file zone is disproportionately thin relative to the four active cross-package boundaries (hench→rex, web→rex, web→sourcevision, cli.js→all packages); as each boundary evolves independently the integration test gap widens. [warning] (cross-package-integration-tests)
- monorepo-root → hench-agent boundary is enforced exclusively via spawn (child_process), not module imports. This means the zone graph has no edge between them despite tight behavioral coupling — zone cohesion/coupling metrics alone cannot detect spawn-based dependencies and should be supplemented with process-level dependency audits. [info] (monorepo-root)
- rex-runtime-data acts as a shared-state bus between hench-agent, web-viewer, and monorepo-root via disk I/O rather than module imports. This file-based coupling is structurally invisible to Louvain zone detection and creates real concurrency hazards (documented in CLAUDE.md) that no import-graph metric can surface. [warning] (rex-runtime-data)
- web-dashboard has 21 outgoing cross-zone calls but 0 incoming, confirming it is the composition root; any future zone that imports from web-dashboard would invert this pattern and should be treated as a critical boundary violation. [info] (web-dashboard)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 79 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 19 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (architecture-docs)
- The breadth of analysis docs (signal handling, memory risks, resource allocation, process lifecycle) reflects thorough architectural auditing; ensure each report links to the PRD item or commit that resolved the finding to close the feedback loop. [info] (architecture-docs)
- Zero cohesion and coupling are expected and correct for a documentation-only zone — no structural action needed. [info] (architecture-docs)
- docs/decisions/ (ADRs) and docs/architecture/ together form a two-tier documentation system — confirming that new architectural decisions are recorded here before implementation would prevent undocumented drift. [info] (architecture-docs)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirms the e2e suite is fully isolated from production code modules, relying only on CLI spawn behavior — this is the correct pattern for orchestration-tier tests. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- architecture-policy.test.js enforcing gateway and tier-boundary rules at the e2e layer is a strong architectural safeguard; it should be run in CI before any merge to main to catch boundary violations early. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- cli-delegation.test.js validates that orchestration scripts spawn sub-CLIs rather than importing them directly — this is the automated guard for the spawn-vs-gateway decision rule and must remain in the suite as packages evolve. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (cross-package-integration-tests)
- With only 2 files this zone is thin; as the integration surface grows (e.g. hench↔rex, web↔rex) additional contract tests should land here rather than in e2e to keep fast feedback loops. [info] (cross-package-integration-tests)
- ... and 21 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "hench-agent" has files across 31 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (hench-agent)
- Add a zone hint explicitly assigning packages/web/src/landing/index.html to web-dashboard. HTML asset files have no import edges and Louvain cannot place them by import affinity, making their zone assignment non-deterministic across analysis runs and polluting cohesion metrics of whichever residual zone they land in. [info] (hench-agent)
- Verify that packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts has active consumers inside the hench package. Its presence in a low-cohesion residual zone may indicate it is imported by fewer modules than expected for a store-layer file. If consumer count is zero or one, consider merging it into an adjacent store file to strengthen the internal connectivity that keeps it within the hench-agent zone boundary. [warning] (hench-agent)
- Zone "web-dashboard" has files across 26 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (web-dashboard)
- The gateway re-export-only contract is machine-enforced for web's gateways via domain-isolation.test.js, but hench's rex-gateway.ts has no equivalent enforcement test. The asymmetry means a logic-bearing re-export added to hench's gateway passes all CI checks undetected. Add a parallel gateway-isolation test inside the hench package or extend architecture-policy.test.js to cover all gateway files across all packages. [warning] (web-dashboard)
- Zone ID 'web-dashboard' does not match the package directory name 'web'. In diagnostic output that reports both package names and zone IDs (e.g. sourcevision health reports, CI summaries), these identifiers appear as unrelated entities. Adopt a convention — either name zones after their package (web) or suffix all zones consistently (web-dashboard → @n-dx/web) — and enforce it in the zone detection pipeline. [info] (web-dashboard)
- Add a standardized 'Resolution' section to each docs/analysis/ audit document linking to the PRD item ID or commit SHA that addressed the finding. Without this link, resolved findings are indistinguishable from open ones in future analysis passes, causing repeat triage effort. [info] (architecture-docs)
- Zone "Architecture Docs" (architecture-docs) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds [info] (architecture-docs)
- No e2e test asserts that package-tier modules (rex, sourcevision, hench) never shell-exec or spawn orchestration-tier scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js). While import-graph enforcement catches module-import inversions, process-spawn inversions from packages back into orchestration scripts are undetectable by static analysis. Add a test in cli-e2e-tests that scans package src/ directories for shell-exec or spawn calls targeting the monorepo root scripts. [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- config.js is the only orchestration-tier script with a documented exception to the spawn-only rule (it reads package config files directly). This exception has no automated test — a future refactor converting config.js to spawn-based delegation would silently break cross-package atomic config reads without failing any test. Add a test case to cli-e2e-tests that exercises a cross-package config read and asserts the result is atomically consistent (e.g. both rex and hench config values appear in a single invocation output). [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- ... and 32 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
